{{Chess Opening Theory/Position|= |Alekhine Defence:Exchange variation| |rd|nd|bd|qd|kd|bd| |rd|= |pd|pd|pd| |pd|pd|pd|pd|= | |nd| |pl| | | | |= | | | | | | | | |= | | |pl|pl| | | | |= | | | | | | | | |= |pl|pl| | | |pl|pl|pl|= |rl|nl|bl|ql|kl|bl|nl|rl|= || }} = Alekhine Defence:Exchange variation = Black must decide whether he will take back with the c pawn : [[/5...cxd6/]] or with the e pawn : [[/5...exd6/]]. In the first case, Black will have a majority of pawns in the center, which may be useful for a later e5 push. In the second case, the game will be more positional, relying more on the pieces placements than on the pawns fight. ==Theory table== {{Chess Opening Theory/Table}}. '1.e4 Nf6 2.e5 Nd5 3.d4 d6 4.c4 Nb6 5.exd6'
